title: dryas punctata subsp. alaskensis
intro: Dryas punctata subsp. alaskensis, also known as Mountain Avens or Alaskan Dryad, is a flowering plant species within the rose family. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits associated with Dryas punctata subsp. alaskensis include treating wounds, reducing inflammation, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be found in the form of infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and interactions.
